question.text: Within what period from the date of valuation must the NAV of an InvIT be disclosed to the stock exchange(s)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, the Investment Manager shall ensure that computation and declaration of NAV of the InvIT based on the valuation done by the valuer shall be disclosed to the stock exchange(s), not later than fifteen days from the date of valuation. **The Trap:** 30 days is the timeline for quarterly reports to the trustee, not for NAV disclosure. 7 working days applies to reporting material facts. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NAV disclosure timeline (15 days from valuation) is the same as the valuation report filing timeline (15 days from receipt), creating a consistent disclosure framework.
